name: Can I get workers comp if I fell from a ladder on the job?

mainEntity.name: Can I get workers comp if I fell from a ladder on the job?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, workers' comp covers ladder falls at work on a no-fault basis for medical and wages.[1][2]
acceptedAnswer.description:
Yes, you are generally eligible for workers' compensation benefits for a ladder fall at work, regardless of fault.[1][2]

This no-fault system covers medical costs, partial lost wages, and disability benefits for job-related injuries like fractures or spinal damage from falls.[1] Coverage applies even if you contributed to the accident.

- Injury must arise from employment duties.
- Report within your state's deadline, often 30 days.
- Benefits exclude pain and suffering.

Notify your employer immediately and file the claim form; a lawyer can maximize benefits and check for additional lawsuits.[2] Early action preserves your rights.

description: Yes, workers' comp covers ladder falls at work on a no-fault basis for medical and wages.[1][2]
